title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted portable binary representation codec, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in portable binary representation codec and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Explicit Clearing
steps.instruction:
Order the Explicit Clearing evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Explicit Clearing.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Explicit Clearing assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Explicit Clearing, keep Explicit Clearing connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug native-systems team handles memory case 4668. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Explicit Clearing has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Explicit Clearing

For this debug demonstration of cmem-explicit-clearing, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-explicit-clearing-gzjsdf */" for changed case 9009. It must encode values without copying native padding, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
